2604.02837.title: Towards Secure Agent Skills: Architecture, Threat Taxonomy, and Security Analysis
2604.02837.paper_summary: Agent Skills is an emerging open standard that defines a modular, filesystem-based packaging format enabling LLM-based agents to acquire domain-specific expertise on demand. Despite rapid adoption across multiple agentic platforms and the emergence of large community marketplaces, the security properties of Agent Skills have not been systematically studied. This paper presents the first comprehensive security analysis of the Agent Skills framework. We define the full lifecycle of an Agent Skill across four phases -- Creation, Distribution, Deployment, and Execution -- and identify the structural attack surface each phase introduces. Building on this lifecycle analysis, we construct a threat taxonomy comprising seven categories and seventeen scenarios organized across three attack layers, grounded in both architectural analysis and real-world evidence. We validate the taxonomy through analysis of five confirmed security incidents in the Agent Skills ecosystem. Based on these findings, we discuss defense directions for each threat category, identify open research challenges, and provide actionable recommendations for stakeholders. Our analysis reveals that the most severe threats arise from structural properties of the framework itself, including the absence of a data-instruction boundary, a single-approval persistent trust model, and the lack of mandatory marketplace security review, and cannot be addressed through incremental mitigations alone.
2604.02837.paper_summary_zh: 代理技能是一種新興的開放標準，定義了一種模組化的基於檔案系統的包裝格式，使基於 LLM 的代理能夠按需獲取特定領域的專業知識。儘管在多個代理平台上迅速採用並出現了大型社區市場，但代理技能的安全性特徵尚未進行系統性的研究。本文呈現了對代理技能框架的首次全面安全分析。我們定義了代理技能的完整生命周期，分為四個階段——創建、分發、部署和執行——並確定每個階段引入的結構性攻擊面。在此生命周期分析的基礎上，我們構建了一個威脅分類法，包含七個類別和十七個場景，組織在三個攻擊層次上，並基於架構分析和現實世界的證據。通過分析代理技能生態系統中的五個確認安全事件，我們驗證了該分類法。根據這些發現，我們討論了每個威脅類別的防禦方向，確定了開放的研究挑戰，並為利益相關者提供了可行的建議。我們的分析顯示，最嚴重的威脅來自於框架本身的結構性特徵，包括缺乏數據-指令邊界、單一批准的持久信任模型以及缺乏強制性的市場安全審查，這些問題無法僅通過逐步的緩解措施來解決。
2604.02837.author: Zhiyuan Li et.al.
2604.02837.authors: Zhiyuan Li, Jingzheng Wu, Xiang Ling, Xing Cui, Tianyue Luo
